key_insight: A neural operator preconditioner trained on mixed Helmholtz datasets can act as an effective nonlinear preconditioner inside FGMRES, enabling robust solves for difficult transcranial ultrasound systems.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical path to accelerate large-scale, patient-specific acoustic simulations where standard iterative solvers struggle with strong heterogeneity and poor convergence.
method: Train a neural operator on coarse-grid Helmholtz problems using a physics-informed relative-residual loss.
Use six mixed velocity-source datasets with randomized sources, idealized skull fields, and noise to improve generalization.
Embed the learned operator as a nonlinear preconditioner in flexible GMRES (FGMRES).
evidence_signal: In numerical tests, the hybrid solver converged on grids 64× larger than training resolution, while classical GMRES and the learned optimizer did not converge within similar computational budgets.
watch_item: Performance depends on dataset design and may be sensitive to how well the training mixtures match real patient-specific velocity and source variability.
paper_title: Neural operator preconditioning from mixed dataset for the Helmholtz equations: Application to transcranial ultrasound
